coin_profile.name: Sushi
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Sushi (SUSHI) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #414 by market cap.

SUSHI.name: Sushi

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (deep bear) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 7.2/10 and survivability 50.0%. Base: 10.0% p.a., Bull: 17.0% p.a., Bear: -15.8% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (10.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (7.2/10). Bull (17.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-15.8%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 50.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

SUSHI.iv_method: Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark

watchlist_signal.reason: A Safe-rated Cash-Flow Protocol trading near or below its speculative fair value — strong quality at a favourable entry point.

bull_points: Current market fear environment may create a near-term mean reversion opportunity in Sushi
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — Sushi's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Up 18% in the past week — rapid short-term move increases likelihood of a pullback before the next leg higher
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, Sushi may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: MC/Fees of 2x is low for a Cash-Flow Protocol — the protocol is priced attractively relative to its actual fee revenue
Multi-year on-chain fee data confirms the protocol has generated real revenue across both bull and bear market cycles
Quality score of 7.2/10 (Safe) places Sushi among the better-quality protocols in the crypto universe
bear_points: FDV/Holders Revenue of 815x means almost none of the protocol's fees flow to token holders — the token lacks direct cash-flow backing
Technology or regulatory disruption risk cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Fee Growth is a clear strength, placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: Capital Efficiency is a clear strength (2.29 MC/fees ratio), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Holder Value Accrual is solid (15.7 % of fees to holders), meeting the expectations you would expect for a protocol at this stage.
metric_assessments.network_demand: Network Demand is a clear strength (17.0 volume/MC %), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Dilution Control is a clear strength (93.65 circulating %), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Protocol Maturity is a concern (414 MC rank). This dimension is below par and worth monitoring closely.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Sushi earns a Safe rating because its fundamentals score 7.2/10 as a Cash-Flow Protocol, above the threshold that distinguishes established protocols from speculative ones. Its standout strength is fee growth. The relatively controlled token supply reduces dilution risk.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Sushi is a Cash-Flow Protocol with strong fundamentals, scoring 7.2/10 overall. Its clearest strength is fee growth, while protocol maturity remains the main area to watch. Key risks: low protocol maturity score (1/10). Sushi is suitable for investors seeking more established crypto exposure with lower protocol-level risk.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Sushi scores 7.2/10 (Safe) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: lium
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.2/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
